source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design a single articulating landing-leg subsystem of a reusable sub-orbital technology-demonstrator rocket as a multi-part telescoping assembly consisting of the titanium outer tube, the titanium inner (telescoping) tube, the footpad face-sheet skin + honeycomb core sandwich, the upper deployment pin joint (pivot into vehicle skirt), the lower ground-contact pin joint (inner tube to footpad), and the telescoping-stroke retention hardware. Framed as a student aerospace-structures paper benchmark. Vehicle dry mass 3000 kg, landing wet mass (with residual propellant) 3500 kg, touchdown velocity envelope -3 m/s vertical + 1 m/s horizontal. Four-leg configuration, each leg supports 3500/4 = 875 kg nominally plus asymmetric landing factor 2x = 1750 kg per leg dynamic. Leg is a telescoping tube with main outer tube 100 mm OD x 90 mm ID x 1800 mm long, inner tube 85 mm OD x 80 mm ID x 1200 mm stroke, with a deployable footpad of 400 mm diameter at the tip. Materials: outer/inner tubes are Titanium 6Al-4V STA (Young's modulus 113.8 GPa, Poisson 0.342, yield 860 MPa, ultimate 950 MPa, density 4430 kg/m^3). Footpad: 2024-T3 Al honeycomb with face sheets 2 mm and core thickness 50 mm for impact crush energy absorption. Load cases: (LC1) nominal touchdown 3 m/s: kinetic energy of 3500/4 = 875 kg at 3 m/s = 3940 J per leg, absorbed by 200 mm crush stroke in honeycomb at constant crush force 19.7 kN; 19.7 kN axial on the telescoping tube; (LC2) asymmetric 3-leg touchdown: one leg carries 50% of vehicle wet weight = 1750 x 9.81 = 17.2 kN vertical per leg + lateral 5 kN; (LC3) ascent vibration: liftoff 3 g axial on leg mass ~40 kg; (LC4) aero re-entry heating: outer tube skin 350 C for 90 s during descent through peak heating; (LC5) fatigue: 100 flight cycles of 0 to LC1 peak load. Pass/fail: (R1) tubes survive LC1 with max von Mises below 633 MPa (ultimate 950 / 1.5); (R2) honeycomb crush force remains within 18-22 kN during LC1 stroke (target 3940 J absorbed at 200 mm); (R3) asymmetric LC2 lateral stability: no buckling of outer tube (linear eigenvalue >= 2.0); (R4) hot-tube yield: at 350 C Ti-6Al-4V yield drops to ~680 MPa, max stress under LC3 must remain below 453 MPa (680/1.5); (R5) leg subsystem mass <= 55 kg per leg; (R6) 100-cycle fatigue alternating stress at pin joints <= 200 MPa. Verification is performed on the assembled outer-tube + inner-tube + footpad + pin-joints multi-body model with representative sliding contact + pin stiffness; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, buckling eigenvalue, honeycomb crush analysis, and mass + thermal report.
short_prompt:
Design a single articulating landing-leg subsystem of a reusable sub-orbital technology-demonstrator rocket, framed as a student aerospace-structures paper benchmark.

Vehicle dry mass 3000 kg, landing wet mass 3500 kg, touchdown velocity envelope -3 m/s vertical + 1 m/s horizontal. Four-leg configuration, each leg supports 3500/4 = 875 kg nominally plus asymmetric landing factor 2x = 1750 kg per leg dynamic. Leg is a telescoping tube with main outer tube 100 mm OD x 90 mm ID x 1800 mm long, inner tube 85 mm OD x 80 mm ID x 1200 mm stroke, with a deployable footpad of 400 mm diameter at the tip.

Materials: outer/inner tubes Titanium 6Al-4V STA (Young's modulus 113.8 GPa, Poisson 0.342, yield 860 MPa, ultimate 950 MPa, density 4430 kg/m^3). Footpad: 2024-T3 Al honeycomb with face sheets 2 mm and core thickness 50 mm.

Load cases: LC1 nominal touchdown 3 m/s: kinetic energy of 3500/4 = 875 kg at 3 m/s = 3940 J per leg, absorbed by 200 mm crush stroke at constant crush force 19.7 kN; 19.7 kN axial on the telescoping tube; LC2 asymmetric 3-leg touchdown 17.2 kN vertical + lateral 5 kN; LC3 ascent vibration liftoff 3 g axial on leg mass ~40 kg; LC4 aero re-entry heating 350 C for 90 s; LC5 fatigue 100 flight cycles of 0 to LC1 peak load.

prompt.title: Reusable rocket landing leg (multi-part telescoping assembly)
prompt.brief: Ti-6Al-4V telescoping leg: outer tube + inner tube + Al honeycomb footpad + upper deployment pin + lower contact pin + stroke retention hardware, for 3 m/s touchdown.

parts.name: outer_tube
parts.role: Ti-6Al-4V outer telescoping tube, 100 mm OD x 90 mm ID x 1800 mm

parts.name: inner_tube
parts.role: Ti-6Al-4V inner telescoping tube, 85 mm OD x 80 mm ID x 1200 mm stroke

parts.name: footpad_sandwich
parts.role: 2024-T3 face-sheet + Al honeycomb crush footpad, 400 mm diameter (absorbs 3940 J at 200 mm stroke)

parts.name: upper_deployment_pin_joint
parts.role: pin joint pivoting the outer tube's upper end into the vehicle skirt

parts.name: lower_ground_contact_pin_joint
parts.role: pin joint between the inner tube's lower end and the footpad sandwich

parts.name: stroke_retention_hardware
parts.role: retention / guide hardware (bushings, stops) constraining the telescoping stroke between outer and inner tubes

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: ~2.2 m deployed length x 400 mm footpad
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: +Z along leg axis, footpad at ground end

boundary_conditions.interfaces: inner tube slides inside outer tube with bronze-bushing retention hardware; upper deployment pin connects outer tube to vehicle skirt; lower contact pin connects inner tube to footpad sandwich
boundary_conditions.support: upper deployment pin fixed to vehicle skirt in all LCs; footpad ground-contact reactions in LC1-LC2

prompt.objective: minimize per-leg mass under 55 kg while passing yield, crush, buckling, hot-tube, and pin-joint fatigue gates

interfaces.load_path: inner tube telescoping inside outer tube with bushing-mediated sliding contact
outer tube upper end pinned to vehicle skirt via deployment pin joint
inner tube lower end pinned to footpad sandwich via contact pin joint
footpad sandwich bonded to lower pin joint interface
stroke retention hardware constraining outer/inner tube telescoping travel

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Reusable rocket landing legs are telescoping tube assemblies with dedicated upper deployment pin and lower contact pin, plus a honeycomb-crush footpad; touchdown, asymmetric landing, and pin-joint fatigue gates all cross sliding and pinned interfaces that must be modelled explicitly.
notes.speculation_caveats: Stroke retention hardware and pin-joint detail are speculative; tubes/footpad material, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
